req.title: Joint account operations (negative variant)
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-068 covers joint-account operations with four distinct risk areas: (1) valid primary-holder transactions, (2) secondary-holder ownership-change attempts, (3) insufficient-funds rejections, and (4) limit-boundary transactions. I model these with EP (valid partition for primary holder), NEG (secondary holder ownership change → 403, insufficient funds → 422/400), BVA (transaction exactly at daily limit), and DT (decision table pairing holder role × operation type). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down account state, and idempotent because every test uses a fresh account ID scoped to its run.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_transaction_succeeds
preconditions: A joint account exists with sufficient balance (>=100.00)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a valid debit transaction of 100.00
expected: Response body contains updated balance reflecting the debit
Transaction ID is present in the response
trace: R-FIN-068: joint-account holder shall perform transactions
EP: primary holder with valid amount belongs to the 'authorised + sufficient funds' equivalence class

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_ownership_change_returns_403
preconditions: A different user ID is available to act as a new owner candidate
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with an ownership-change payload targeting a new primary owner
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating insufficient privileges
trace: R-FIN-068: secondary holder shall NOT be able to modify ownership
R-FIN-068 error pathway: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change
NEG: unauthorised actor in the 'disallowed operation' partition

tests.name: test_transaction_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary
preconditions: A joint account exists with balance equal to the per-day limit (e.g. 10000.00)
No prior transactions have been made today on this account
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a debit equal to the exact daily limit (10000.00)
Attempt a second POST /accounts/{id}/joint with amount 0.01 (limit + 1 cent)
expected: First request returns HTTP 200/201 and the transaction succeeds
Second request returns HTTP 422 or 429 indicating limit exceeded
Balance after first transaction reflects the full debit
trace: R-FIN-068: exceeded limits must be tested
BVA: at-limit value (10000.00) and just-over-limit value (10000.01) bracket the daily cap boundary

tests.name: test_insufficient_funds_debit_rejected
preconditions: A joint account exists with a balance of 50.00
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with a debit of 100.00 (exceeds balance)
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity or 400 Bad Request is returned
Response body contains an error code for insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-068: insufficient funds negative path must be tested
NEG: debit amount > current balance belongs to the 'insufficient funds' error partition
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

def test_insufficient_funds_debit_rejected(
    joint_account_low_balance_fixture, primary_holder_token
):
    account_id = joint_account_low_balance_fixture["account_id"]
    initial_balance = joint_account_low_balance_fixture["initial_balance"]
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {primary_holder_token}"}
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": initial_balance + 50.00, "currency": "USD"}

    response = requests.post(
        f"{<BASE_URL>}/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert response.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Expected 400/422, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body, "Missing error descriptor in response"

    balance_check = requests.get(
        f"{<BASE_URL>}/accounts/{account_id}/joint",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<ADMIN_TOKEN>}"},
    )
    assert balance_check.json()["balance"] == pytest.approx(initial_balance, abs=0.01)

tests.name: test_decision_table_holder_role_vs_operation
preconditions: Both primary and secondary holder credentials are available
Account has sufficient balance for valid transactions
actions: For each (role, operation) pair in the decision table, send the corresponding API request
Collect HTTP status codes and compare against expected outcomes
trace: R-FIN-068: joint-account holder shall perform transactions; secondary shall not modify ownership
DT: exhaustive pairing of {primary, secondary} x {debit, ownership-change} decision columns
